evals.expected_output: Full pipeline execution on the included test fixture (Express app with 6 planted bugs). Should run Recon -> Hunter -> Skeptic -> Referee and produce a final report confirming at least 5 of 6 planted bugs with severity ratings, file paths, and suggested fixes.

assertions.text: Pipeline runs all phases: Recon, Hunter, Skeptic, Referee
At least 5 of 6 planted bugs are confirmed in the final report
Each confirmed bug includes file path, line numbers, severity, and suggested fix
False positives are challenged and filtered by the Skeptic/Referee pipeline
Final report includes scan metadata (mode, files scanned, coverage)
Fix pipeline is triggered by default when confirmed bugs exist; only --scan-only disables fixes

evals.expected_output: Single-file mode scan of an auth file. Should skip Recon (not needed for single file), run one Hunter, one Skeptic, and one Referee. Output should focus on security and logic bugs in the auth file specifically.

assertions.text: Selects single-file mode (1 file detected)
Skips Recon agent (not needed for single-file mode)
Hunter scans the target file and reports findings with BUG-ID format
Skeptic challenges the findings with code-based counter-arguments
Referee produces a final verdict (REAL BUG or NOT A BUG) for each finding

evals.expected_output: Branch diff mode. Should run git diff to find changed files between feature-auth and develop branches, filter out non-source files, then run the full pipeline on the changed source files.

assertions.text: Runs git diff --name-only to extract changed files between branches
Filters out non-source files (configs, docs, assets, lockfiles)
Reports the number of source files to scan after filtering
Selects appropriate mode based on file count (small, parallel, extended, etc.)

evals.expected_output: Staged file mode for pre-commit checking. Should run git diff --cached --name-only to get staged files, filter non-source files, then scan the staged source files.

assertions.text: Runs git diff --cached --name-only to get staged files
Filters out non-source files from the staged list
Scans full file contents of staged files (not just diffs)

evals.expected_output: Full pipeline with auto-fix. After Phase 1 (find & verify), should proceed to Phase 2: create a git branch, acquire single-writer lock, detect test infrastructure, capture test baseline, run Fixer clusters sequentially with checkpoint commits, run post-fix tests, auto-revert regressions, and release lock.

assertions.text: Creates a git safety branch (bug-hunter-fix-*) before applying fixes
Detects test command from package.json or project config
Captures test baseline before applying fixes
Fixer agents implement minimal, surgical code changes
Each fix is a separate checkpoint commit with descriptive message
Post-fix test run compares against baseline (new failures vs pre-existing)
Fixes that cause new test failures are auto-reverted
Acquires and releases .claude/bug-hunter-fix.lock around fix phase
Auto-fixes only bugs that pass confidence eligibility threshold

evals.expected_output: Loop mode for thorough coverage. Should create ralph-loop state files, iterate the pipeline until all queued scannable source files are scanned, track canonical coverage in .bug-hunter/coverage.json, render .bug-hunter/coverage.md from it, and mark ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE when done.

assertions.text: Creates .claude/ralph-loop.local.md state file for loop mode
Creates or updates .bug-hunter/coverage.json as the canonical loop state and renders .bug-hunter/coverage.md from it
Tracks file coverage status per iteration in coverage.json
Subsequent iterations only scan uncovered files (no re-scanning DONE files)
Marks ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE only when all queued scannable files show done

evals.prompt: Can you check my Express API for security vulnerabilities? The code is in src/
evals.expected_output: Should trigger the bug-hunter skill (even though the user didn't say /bug-hunter) and run a security-focused scan on the src/ directory. The deep Hunter should prioritize security findings, with optional triage hints when enabled.

assertions.text: Triggers bug-hunter skill from natural language (security audit request)
Runs Recon to map architecture and identify trust boundaries
Deep Hunter focuses on injection, auth bypass, input validation, and secrets exposure in security audit requests
Output includes severity ratings (Critical, Medium, Low) for each finding
Framework-specific protections are checked (Express middleware, helmet, etc.)

evals.expected_output: Fix mode with approval. Should find bugs in auth directory, then fix them but prompt the user before each edit (approval mode). Fixer agents run in default mode rather than auto mode.

assertions.text: APPROVE_MODE is set to true from --approve flag
Fixer agents run in mode: default (user reviews each edit)
Reports 'Running in approval mode' to the user
Fixes are still committed as individual checkpoint commits

evals.expected_output: Large-repo run should initialize .claude/bug-hunter-state.json, split files into sequential chunks, and resume from state if interrupted.

assertions.text: Initializes bug-hunter-state.json with chunk metadata
Processes chunks sequentially and marks each chunk state
Can resume from existing state file without rescanning completed chunks

evals.expected_output: Hash cache should skip unchanged files and focus scan effort on changed files only.

assertions.text: Runs hash-filter against bug-hunter-state.json before deep scan
Reports skipped unchanged files from cache

evals.expected_output: Pipeline should fail fast before spawning subagents when payload validation fails.

assertions.text: Validates payload via payload-guard.cjs before each subagent launch
Does not launch subagent when payload validation fails

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter --fix src/ while another fix run is active
evals.expected_output: Fix phase should stop when single-writer lock cannot be acquired.

assertions.text: Attempts to acquire .claude/bug-hunter-fix.lock before any edits
Stops Phase 2 with clear lock-held message when lock is already held

evals.expected_output: Auto-fix should run only on high-confidence bugs and leave low-confidence bugs as manual review.

assertions.text: Applies confidence threshold gating (>=75%) for auto-fix eligibility
Reports low-confidence bugs as manual-review and does not auto-edit them

evals.prompt: /bug-hunter src/ on a CLI without spawn_agent
evals.expected_output: Pipeline should auto-select the available orchestration backend and continue. If remote orchestration is unavailable, it should fall back to local sequential execution.

assertions.text: Selects AGENT_BACKEND in preflight based on available runtime tools
Falls back to next backend when launch fails
Completes run with local-sequential fallback when no delegation backend is available

evals.expected_output: Orchestrator should enforce per-chunk timeout, retry failed chunk once with backoff, and persist attempt details in run journal.

assertions.text: Uses run-bug-hunter.cjs for autonomous chunk orchestration
Retries timed out/failed chunk according to max-retries and backoff policy
Writes attempt events to .claude/bug-hunter-run.log
